title: Aspherical dome display and method of rear projection
abstract: Aspherical dome display and method of rear projection Embodiments and methods described herein concern a non-spherical projection display 5 structure and system that can be optimized for the ergonomics of preferably one viewer inside the display structure or system. The structure or system is preferably for use with widescreen projectors. The present application is aimed at creating a visual projection display and method of operating the display that has a wide horizontal and vertical field of view and uses a rear projected display structure having a projection screen surface with a 10 thickness. The display structure has at least one section with a monotonically increasing or decreasing radius of curvature.
claims:
Claims Claims A visual system comprising a non-spherical dome display screen; wherein the non- spherical shape of the screen is such that it comprises an axis of revolution preferably perpendicular to a horizontal plane and substantially forms a surface of revolution around said axis of revolution and has a thickness, said surface of revolution being formed by the rotation around the axis of revolution of a planar curve, each point on the planar curve being defined by an angle and a radius, the plane curve having at least one section where the radius of curvature increases or decreases monotonically with change of said angle. A visual system according to claim 1 wherein the non- spherical shape of the screen has a minimum radius of curvature Rl and a maximum radius of curvature R2 where R2 differs from Rl and where the non- spherical shape of the screen has been optimized for the viewer' s ergonomics by dR integrating the radius of curvature of the screen surface through the formulasâ = dR C 2 + e (d>, Î) andâ = C 1 Â± e (d>, Î) where C 1 and C 2 are constants and Î²(Ï,Î¸) is do a variable tolerance factor on these constants that is in absolute values smaller than C 1 and C 2 for all Î and Ï values and where Î is the angle of the screen point P in the horizontal plane and Ï is the zenith angle, or by integrating the radius of dR dR curvature of the screen surface through the formulasâ = C ? + e andâ = + e where C lt C 2 and e are constants and Î is the angle of the screen point P in the horizontal plane and Ï is the zenith angle. A visual system according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the non- spherical shape of the screen has a first arc and a second arc with respectively a first and second radius of curvature Rl and R2, wherein the first arc is located in the upper part of the screen and the second arc is located in the lower part of the screen, and is such that R1<R2. 4. A visual system according to any previous claim, wherein the screen is surrounded by at least a first group of projectors configured to display images for a user located inside the non- spherical dome screen , wherein the first group of projectors comprises 

 at least 3 projectors arranged around the periphery of the screen in a horizontal plane so as to provide a combined image having a horizontal field of view of at least 180Â° and a vertical field of view in the range of at least 20Â° downward to 60Â° upward. 5. A visual system according to claim 4, further comprising a second group of projectors (3), wherein the second group of projectors comprises at least one projector configured to illuminate the upper part of the screen, and provides an additional vertical field of view up to 90Â° being the zenith. A visual system according to any of the claims 2 to 5, wherein the variation of the dR radius of curvature along the planar curveâ = C 2 Â± e(0, Î) or Â± e wherein C 2 is a constant in the range of 0.5% and 5% of the radius R2 per degree and where the absolute value of e or less than 2% of R2 per degree. A visual system according to any of the claims 2 to 5, wherein the variation of the dR radius of curvature along the azimuthal angle is given byâ = C 1 Â± e (d>, Î) or Â± e do wherein C^is a constant in the range of 0% to 2.5% of the radius R2 per degree and where the absolute value of e or less than 2% of R2 per degree. A visual system according to any of the claims 4 to 7, wherein the first group of projectors comprises at most nine projectors. 9. A visual system according to any of the claims 5 to 8, wherein the second group of projectors comprises at most four projectors. 10. A visual system according to any of the claims 4 to 9, further comprising mirrors associated to the first and/or a second group of projectors so as to fold the light beam projected by the projectors. 11. A visual system according to any of the previous claims, wherein the vertical field of view is at most of -50Â° downward and a full 90Â° upward with 2 layers of projectors. 12. A visual system according to any of the claims 4 to 11, wherein the first group of projectors are arranged so as to cover a horizontal field of view up to 360Â°. 13. A visual system according to any of the claims 2 to 12, wherein C^is preferably as small as possible having a value different from zero determined by the space constraints of the location in which the system needs to be installed. 14. A visual system of any of the claims 2 to 13, having one layer of projectors with each projector covering between 40Â° and 90Â° horizontal field of view with between four and nine projectors to cover the total horizontal field of view. 15. A visual system of claim 14, that has two layers of projectors, one layer around the periphery with each projector covering between 40Â° and 90Â° horizontal field of view and 1 layer of projectors on the zenith area covering at least the screen part that has a zenith angle between 75Â° and 90Â°. 16. A visual system of claim 15 that has but-matched projectors with a geometry but- matching accuracy of better than 10 arc minutes as seen from the center of the dome. 17. A visual system of any of the claims 2 to 16 that has projectors configured to provide overlapping blended images. 18. A non-spherical dome screen for use in rear projection for a user located inside the dome screen, wherein - the shape of the screen is such that it comprises an axis of revolution preferably perpendicular to a horizontal plane and substantially forms a solid of revolution around said axis of revolution having an angle of curvature greater than 180Â°, said solid of revolution having a minimum radius of curvature Rl and a maximum radius of curvature R2 where R2 differs from Rl and where the non-spherical screen shape has been optimized for the viewer's ergonomics, the screen comprising a first layer and a second diffusing layer on the first layer, the second diffusing layer containing a light absorbing material and light diffusing 

 particles, the second diffusing layer being adapted to have a value of the product of the absorption coefficient and thickness of between 0.1 and 5. 19. The non-spherical dome screen of claim 18, wherein the second diffusing layer is a synthetic resin layer and the light diffusing particles can be embedded in a resin material. 20. The non-spherical dome screen of claim 1 8 or 19, wherein the second diffusing layer is on the inner surface of the first layer. 2 1 . The non-spherical dome screen of any of the claims 1 8 to 20, wherein the second diffusing layer comprises multiple layers. 22. The non-spherical dome screen of any of the claims 1 8 to 2 1 , wherein the second diffusing layer comprises a mix of diffusing layers and l ight absorbing layers. 23. The non-spherical dome screen of any of the claims 1 8 to 22, wherein the second diffusing layer comprises alternating diffusing layers and light absorbing layers. 24. The non-spherical dome screen of any of the claims 1 8 to 23, wherein the first layer is a synthetic resin layer or is made of glass. 25. The non-spherical dome screen of any of the claims 1 8 to 24, wherein the gain for backseat tering (Gr) has a value Gr < 0. 1 for an angle > 40Â°. 26. The non-spherical dome screen of any of the claims 1 8 to 24, value of Gr can be < 0.07 while ^ > 7 . 27.  radius of curvature R2 where R2 differs from Rl and where the non- spherical screen shape has been optimized for the viewer' s ergonomics: configuring at least a first group of projectors to display images onto the screen for a user located inside the dome screen, back projecting images from the at least first group of projectors the at least first group of projectors comprising at least three projectors arranged around the periphery of the screen in a horizontal plane so as to provide a combined image having a horizontal field of view of at least 180Â° and a vertical field of view in the range of at least 20Â° downward to 60Â° upward. 28. Method according to claim 27 or 28, wherein the non- spherical dome display screen has a non-spherical shape of the screen such that it comprises an axis of revolution perpendicular to a horizontal plane and substantially forms a surface of revolution around said axis of revolution and has a thickness, said surface of revolution being formed by the rotation around the axis of revolution of a planar curve, each point on the planar curve being defined by an angle and a radius, the planar curve having at least one section where the radius of curvature increases or decreases monotonically with change of said angle. 30. Method according to claim 27, wherein integration of the radius of curvature of the dR dR screen surface is through the formulasâ = C ? + e andâ = + e where C lt C 2 and e are constants and Î is the angle of the screen point P in the horizontal plane and Ï is the zenith angle. 3 1 . Method according to claim 27, wherein configuring the variation of the radius of dR curvature along the plane curve is given byâ = C 2 Â± e or Â± Î²(Ï,Î¸) wherein C 2 is a constant in the range of 0.5% and 5% of the radius R2 per degree and e or Î²(Ï,Î¸) is preferably less than 2% of R2 per degree. 32. Method according to claim 27, wherein the variation of the radius of curvature dR along the azimuthal angle is given byâ = C 1 Â± e or +Î²(Ï,Î¸) wherein C^is a constant in the range of 0% to 2.5% of the radius R2 per degree and e or e( Ï,Î¸) is preferably less than 2% of R2 per degree. 33. Method according to claim 27, wherein the non-spherical shape of the screen has a first arc and a second arc with respectively a first and second radius of curvature Rl and R2, wherein the first arc is located in the upper part of the screen and the second arc is located in the lower part of the screen, and is such that R1<R2. 34. Method according to claim 27, wherein the screen shape and its radius of curvature variation parameters C 1 and C 2 are optimized to match wide screen projectors with an aspect ratio larger than 1.34. 35. Method according to any of the claims 28 to 34, wherein the value of C 2 is y determined by comparing the resolution requirement of the system with the resolution of the projectors in one direction optionally along a short axis of the projector image and an overlap required between projectors. 36. Method according to claim 35, wherein the overlap is determined by the blending or edging technology that is being used, whereby both determine how many 

 projectors are needed in the first layer of the system. 37. Method according to any of the claims 28 to 36, wherein the vertical field of view of the system that has to be covered with the first layer of projectors is determined. 38. Method according claim 34, wherein, taking into account the aspect ratio of the widescreen projectors and the opening angle of the projectors towards the vertical field of view, a value for C 2 is determined that matches this aspect ratio and the opening angle with the required vertical field of view of the first layer of projectors. 39. Method according to any of the claims 28 to 38, wherein if a zenith is being used in the visual system, C 2 is further refined in the zenith using the same method on the second layer of projectors until a value is reached that both satisfies the requirements for the entire field of view of the system.
